report_name: Aur0ra Ransomware Claims Attacks on US Agriculture and Canadian Trucking Firms

threat_actors.name: Aur0ra
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that operates a data leak site to conduct double-extortion attacks. The group claimed responsibility for attacks against Allan Brothers, Inc. in the US and Diamond Truck Centres in Canada in June 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data leakage.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Aur0ra ransomware group encrypts victim files to disrupt operations as part of its extortion strategy.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The Aur0ra group exfiltrates victim data and posts it to their data leak site as part of their double-extortion tactics.

malware.name: Aur0ra Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware used by the Aur0ra group to encrypt victim networks and exfiltrate data for double extortion.

identities.name: Allan Brothers, Inc.
identities.description: A U.S.-based agricultural company listed as a victim on the Aur0ra ransomware group's data leak site.

identities.name: Diamond Truck Centres
identities.description: A Canadian commercial vehicle dealership and service provider listed as a victim on the Aur0ra ransomware group's data leak site.

infrastructure.name: Aur0ra Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A data leak site operated by the Aur0ra ransomware group to name victims and leak stolen data as part of their double-extortion model.

indicators.name: Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) Command To Delete Volume Shadow Copies
indicators.pattern:
title: Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) Command To Delete Volume Shadow Copies
id: 9a7b1c3d-e4f5-4a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies. This is a common technique used by ransomware prior to encryption.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-17
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects commands used to delete backups and shadow copies, a common precursor to encryption by ransomware.

indicators.name: PsExec Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PsExec Execution
id: 8b6a0c2d-d3e4-4f7a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a legitimate administrative tool often abused by threat actors for lateral movement.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-17
tags:
  - attack.t1569.002
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName: 'psexec.exe'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith: '\PSTools\PsExec.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects the use of legitimate administrative tools like PsExec for lateral movement, a common tactic for ransomware groups.
